name: subagent-control-protocol
description: Codex plugin for orchestrating structured Claude Code subagents and host-spawned Codex workers from one controller workflow.

interface.shortDescription: Orchestrate Codex-led Claude Code subagents and named Codex workers with structured results.
interface.longDescription: The Subagent Control Protocol plugin lets Codex spawn and supervise Claude Code subagents through a bundled MCP runtime, while its orchestrator Skill also teaches Codex how to route named non-Claude work to host-spawned Codex workers such as huoshan-worker, zhipu-worker, or normal-worker. Each worker returns a structured result describing files changed, commands run, verification evidence, risks, and next steps, enabling deterministic multi-agent orchestration with clear boundary controls. Published as a repo marketplace bundle: the repository root is the marketplace root, and the installable plugin bundle lives at ./plugins/subagent-control-protocol. The runtime launches through ./dist/bootstrap.mjs inside that plugin bundle. The bootstrap can check an update manifest at startup, run the newest verified MCP runtime from a local cache, and fall back to the bundled runtime when offline or pinned.
defaultPrompt: Run a Claude Code subagent that implements the assigned task and returns a structured JSON result.
Dispatch parallel Claude Code subagents with clear boundaries and collect their structured results.
Route named Codex worker aliases such as huoshan or zhipu to host-spawned worker agents, falling back to normal-worker when configured.
Spawn a single Claude Code subagent to verify changes and report evidence, risks, and next steps.
Install or update this plugin from the repo marketplace; the bundled bootstrap can check for a verified MCP runtime update at startup before falling back to ./dist/server.mjs.
